This has created a situation in which many Americans are more likely to seek out information that confirms their pre-existing views, rather than seeking out diverse perspectives and challenging their assumptions.
5. **Erosion of trust in institutions**: The influence of media narratives on public perception has also contributed to the erosion of trust in institutions, including the media, government, and electoral system. This narrative was fueled by Democratic gains in the House of Representatives and the Senate, as well as the election of several Democratic governors and mayors.

This narrative has been thoroughly debunked by fact-checkers and experts, but it has still had a significant impact on public perception of the election results.
4. **The "Fake News" narrative**: Trump and his supporters have often used the term "fake news" to describe media outlets that have reported on the election and his campaign in a way that is critical of him.
